planned_changes: Add an 'Assumptions and Transformations' section to expert_memo.md explicitly defining fifo_full semantics. If fifo_full is to be treated boolean (1==full), show the normalization formula (e.g., normalized_fifo = IF(raw_fifo==1,1,0) or a threshold formula) and justify the choice.

Decide and document the cost model (one-time vs per-row). Update the recommendation table to show aggregated cost math for the chosen model. Provide worked examples: (a) one-time application cost; (b) per-row application cost (e.g., 3 * O1 = 195000) and compare to max_budget.

Export workbook formulas to a new sheet named 'Formulas' containing the exact formula strings for all key cells (gap, fifo_full test, deadlock flag, sensitivity multipliers, option effect calculations, budget aggregation). Also include a plain-text export (formulas.txt) as an artifact for environments without openpyxl.

Update ready_valid_checker_2.xlsx to include the formulas-sheet and a README sheet that documents assumptions, variable names, and how to reproduce calculations. Ensure no workbook features prevent programmatic parsing (avoid macros or proprietary objects).

Fix grader-result metadata inconsistencies: ensure the grader output has a single authoritative passed_criteria and failed_criteria listing and that deterministic_precheck and final fields are consistent. Add a short note in the memo acknowledging the re-evaluation after changes.
Rerun local checks and resubmit the revised attempt. Attach the formulas.txt and updated memo so the verifier can re-run semantic checks and confirm rubric items ri_2 and ri_3 are satisfied.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve semantic ambiguities (fifo_full and cost aggregation), make formulas verifiable without openpyxl, and correct grader result inconsistencies so rubric scoring is unambiguous.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are present and well-documented, but semantic ambiguities and verification gaps reduce confidence. Key issues: (1) fifo_full interpretation is ambiguous (memo tests fifo_full = 1 while packet contains numeric values like 35/31/46) and is not justified or flagged; (2) remediation option costs are not defined as either one-time or per-row, yet the recommendation applies O1 to each row without aggregated cost calculations; (3) the .xlsx could not be programmatically inspected in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable) so in-workbook formulas could not be validated; (4) grader output contains internal inconsistency about which rubric items passed/failed, reducing trust in the reported pass/fail status.
